perturbation.original_text: 1.22 "Net Revenue" means gross revenue less costs not to exceed [*] percent ([*]%) of such gross revenue, including reasonable third-party agency/ third-party advertising sales commissions and rebates and serving, bad debts and applicable taxes.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.22 "Net Revenue" means gross revenue less costs, including agency commissions, rebates and taxes.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies a limit on deductions from gross revenue, capping costs at a certain percentage. The changed text removes this percentage limit, leading to uncertainty in calculating Net Revenue. This creates an in-text contradiction because the subsequent clauses regarding royalty payments and audit, depend on a well-defined Net Revenue to properly calculate fees. Since the Net Revenue can be calculated without a top, it will make the payment for royalties unpredictable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1 Royalties. (i) Royalty Rates. HSWI shall pay to World Book Royalties calculated as a defined percentage of the Net Revenue received by HSWI from advertising generated directly from Content by web pages containing Content or any portion of the Content provided by World Book to HSWI hereunder. The Royalty rate shall be [*] percent ([*]%) on the Affinities and Reference Content; and [*] percent ([*]%) on Translated Content.
perturbation.justification: The original text limits deductions from gross revenue when calculating 'Net Revenue', but the changed text removes this limit. Section 4.1 defines royalties as a percentage of 'Net Revenue'. Without a limit on deductions, the 'Net Revenue' calculation becomes unpredictable, which contradicts the explicit percentage defined for the royalty rate in section 4.1.

perturbation.original_text: 2.5 Exclusivity. Commencing on the Effective Date and continuing for eighteen (18) months, World Book shall work exclusively with HSWI to publish Chinese language Content for the Open Free Web and shall not itself, directly or indirectly, publish Chinese language Content for the Open Free Web. For purposes of clarity, and except as otherwise set forth herein, World Book maintains the right to continue to publish Content and Images for its paid-subscription websites and other existing or new partner paid-subscription websites, provided that such are not within the definition of the Open Free Web. Additionally commencing on the Effective Date and continuing for eighteen (18) months, World Book shall offer HSWI a right of first refusal to create any paid-subscription Chinese language websites, which do not exist as of the date hereof, using the Content on equal or better terms as agreed to between World Book and a third party.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.5 Exclusivity. World Book shall work with HSWI to publish Chinese language Content for the Open Free Web. World Book maintains the right to continue to publish Content and Images for its paid-subscription websites and other existing or new partner paid-subscription websites.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the initial clause that defines the term of the exclusivity (eighteen months commencing on the Effective Date) and the obligation for 'right of first refusal', the modified text contradicts the implicit expectation that the exclusivity is time-limited. Because by removing the term, it creates the assumption that the contract can last indefinitely. The term on which the royalties are based on, are not subject to the agreed term for using the Chinese Content. This perturbation makes it less predictable the income for the services being agreed upon in the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1 Royalties. (i) Royalty Rates. HSWI shall pay to World Book Royalties calculated as a defined percentage of the Net Revenue received by HSWI from advertising generated directly from Content by web pages containing Content or any portion of the Content provided by World Book to HSWI hereunder. The Royalty rate shall be [*] percent ([*]%) on the Affinities and Reference Content; and [*] percent ([*]%) on Translated Content.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies an 18-month exclusivity period for World Book and HSWI, with a right of first refusal for World Book to create paid-subscription Chinese language websites. The changed text removes the 18-month limitation and the right of first refusal. This creates a contradiction with section 4.1, which defines royalty rates for the content provided by World Book. Without the time limitation on exclusivity, the basis on which those royalty rates are determined is undefined, potentially creating an indefinite financial obligation. This contradicts the implicitly time-limited nature of the agreement in the original text and introduces ambiguity regarding the scope and duration of the royalty obligations, which makes it an in-text contradiction as described in the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5. AUDIT. HSWI shall maintain complete and accurate records to support and document the Royalties owed under this Agreement in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles consistently applied and to document compliance with the terms of this Agreement. HSWI shall also provide reasonable assistance to World Book or its designated agent to conduct audits to confirm the payments hereunder. Any such audit will be conducted upon [*] ([*]) days notice and during regular business hours, and shall be at [*] expense, unless such audit reveals a discrepancy of more than [*] percent ([*]%) in the total applicable amount reported by HSWI, in which case [*] shall pay for, or reimburse [*] the cost of, such audit. Any such audit shall be conducted by an independent certified public accounting firm which is not engaged in performing other work for World Book or its affiliates; which agrees to enter into a confidentiality agreement with HSWI; and which is not compensated in any manner of contingency arrangements on the basis of its findings. HSWI further agrees that until the expiration of [*] ([*]) year after the termination of this Agreement, HSWI will make available upon written request to World Book or any of its duly authorized representatives, this Agreement and books, documents, and records of HSWI that are necessary to verify the nature and extent of the revenue derived by HSWI from advertising related to the Content hereunder. No more than [*] audit may be conducted in any [*] month period, unless the then-most-recent audit reveals a discrepancy of more than [*] percent ([*]%) in the total applicable amount reported by HSWI.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. AUDIT. HSWI shall maintain records to support and document the Royalties owed under this Agreement and to document compliance with the terms of this Agreement. HSWI shall also provide assistance to World Book or its designated agent to conduct audits to confirm the payments hereunder. HSWI further agrees that HSWI will make available upon written request to World Book or any of its duly authorized representatives, this Agreement and books, documents, and records of HSWI that are necessary to verify the nature and extent of the revenue derived by HSWI from advertising related to the Content hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: By removing clauses related to the specifics of the audit process, such as advance notice, cost allocation based on discrepancy findings, auditor independence and compensation, and limiting audit frequency, the modified text introduces significant ambiguity and the audit process is not agreed, since the terms are no longer explicit. There is an in-text contradiction because the removal of these specifics makes the enforcement of the audit clause uncertain and subject to dispute. The omission of audit limitations directly impacts the royalty payments, since the way the audit works, may influence in a drastic way the royalty payments. By omitting key points such as audit frequency, this affects section 4.1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1 Royalties. (i) Royalty Rates. HSWI shall pay to World Book Royalties calculated as a defined percentage of the Net Revenue received by HSWI from advertising generated directly from Content by web pages containing Content or any portion of the Content provided by World Book to HSWI hereunder. The Royalty rate shall be [*] percent ([*]%) on the Affinities and Reference Content; and [*] percent ([*]%) on Translated Content.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified the audit process, including notice periods, cost allocation based on discrepancy, auditor independence, and audit frequency limitations. The changed text removes these specifics, making the audit process ambiguous. This creates a contradiction with section 4.1, which defines royalty rates based on revenue. Since the audit process can drastically influence royalty payments, the lack of specifics in the changed text directly impacts the certainty and enforceability of the royalty payments outlined in section 4.1, creating a strong in-text contradiction.
